title: Print all parts
description: 3D print all the plastic parts. Using the 'all parts' STL file is recommended. Infill is 15%, support on built plate only.

title: Press link 1 onto motor 1
description: Press the motor 1 bearing mount onto the GIM6010-8. This is a press-fit part; the extra screw holes are available if you want a more permanent assembly.

title: Insert the motor 1 bearing
description: Seat the 6812 bearing into the motor 1 bearing case. It should be a tight fit.

title: Press on the motor 2 housing
description: Press the motor 2 housing into the bearing and align the three mounting holes with the prongs on motor 1.

title: Screw motor 2 housing to motor 1
description: Fasten the motor 2 housing to the motor 1 shaft with at least three M3 x 8 mm socket screws.

title: Insert motor 2 into the housing
description: Slide motor 2 (STS3095) into the motor 1 housing. Keep the connector facing the same direction shown in the render.

title: Screw housing to motor 2
description: Install at least two of the STS3095 machine screws on each side of the housing.

title: Snap on the link 2 cap
description: Snap the link 2 cap into place to enclose motor 2. Check the orientation before pressing it fully home.

title: Slot link 3 onto motor 2
description: Push link 3 onto the motor 2 gear horns. Follow the groove direction shown in the render and press evenly.

title: Screw link 3 to motor 2
description: Insert the washers first, then fasten link 3 with M3 x 8 mm socket screws. Use thread locker on these screws.

title: Insert motor 3 into link 3
description: Remove the gear horn, then insert motor 3 (STS3250) into the cavity at the end of link 3. Reinstall the horn after the motor is seated.

title: Screw motor 3 to link 3
description: Fasten motor 3 to link 3 using two of the M2 screws supplied with the STS3250.

title: Insert and fasten the motor 3 bearing
description: Press the motor 3 bearing (6806-2RS) into the pocket, then retain it with two M2.5 screws and large-diameter washers.

title: Snap on the link 3 cap
description: Press the link 3 cap firmly into place. Add a small amount of glue if you want a stronger hold.

title: Attach link 4 to link 3
description: Press the connector on link 4 into the bearing in link 3, then rotate link 4 until it sits flush.

title: Screw link 4 to motor 3
description: Fasten link 4 to the motor 3 gear horn with four M3 x 10 mm socket screws and washers. Use thread locker.

title: Insert motor 4 into link 4
description: Insert motor 4 (STS3095) into link 4 with the connector oriented as shown.

title: Screw link 4 housing to motor 4
description: Install at least two of the STS3095 machine screws on each side to lock motor 4 into the housing.

title: Snap on the link 4 cap
description: Snap the link 4 cap into place. Add a small amount of glue if the cap needs extra retention.

title: Slot link 5 onto motor 4
description: Slot link 5 onto the motor 4 gear horn, following the groove direction shown in the render.

title: Screw link 5 to motor 4
description: Insert the washers first, then fasten link 5 to motor 4 with up to eight M3 x 8 mm socket screws.

title: Insert motor 5 into link 5
description: Slide motor 5 (STS3215) into the link 5 cavity until it reaches the end stop.

title: Screw motor 5 to link 5
description: Use two of the STS3215 self-tapping screws to fasten motor 5 to link 5.

title: Insert and fasten the motor 5 bearing
description: Press the 6806-2RS bearing into link 5 and retain it with the M2.5 tapping screws and washers.

title: Snap on the link 5 cap
description: Snap the link 5 cap into place. Use a small amount of glue if the cap needs extra retention.

title: Attach link 6 to link 5
description: Push the link 6 connector into the bearing in link 5, then rotate link 6 until it aligns with link 5.

title: Screw link 6 to motor 5
description: Fasten link 6 to motor 5 with four M3 x 10 mm socket screws and washers. Use thread locker.

title: Insert motor 6 into link 6
description: Insert motor 6 (STS3215) into the link 6 housing with the connector oriented as shown.

title: Screw motor 6 to the link 6 housing
description: Fasten motor 6 to the link 6 housing using the M2 self-tapping screws supplied with the STS3215.

title: Attach link 7 to motor 6
description: Slot link 7 onto the motor 6 gear horn, following the groove direction.

title: Screw link 7 to motor 6
description: Insert the washers first, then fasten link 7 to motor 6 with up to eight M3 x 8 mm socket screws.

title: Insert motor 7 into link 7 housing
description: Press motor 7 (STS3215) into the link 7 housing. The fit is intentionally tight, so keep the motor square as it goes in.

title: Attach link 8 to motor 7
description: Press link 8 onto the horn of motor 7, following the groove direction.

title: Screw link 8 to motor 7
description: Insert the washers first, then fasten link 8 to motor 7 with up to eight M4 x 8 mm socket screws.

title: Assemble the PincOpen gripper
description: Build the PincOpen gripper using the dedicated Pollen Robotics instructions, then bring the completed gripper back to the GEM assembly.

title: Fasten the gripper to link 8
description: Fasten the PincOpen gripper to link 8 with four M2.5 x 10 mm tapping screws. This completes the arm structure.

title: Attach the wrist camera
description: Mount the wrist camera and fasten it with the M2 tapping screws supplied with the STS3215.

title: Check the completed arm
description: Review the full assembly before wiring. Check caps, screws, bearings, cable clearance, and gripper alignment.

title: Mount head and wrist cameras
description: Install the standardized head and wrist cameras so datasets match the expected GEM physical setup.

title: Wire power, bus board, USB, and emergency stop
description: Connect the 24 V PSU, buck converters, serial bus board, USB hub, and emergency stop. Label each motor cable before closing the arm.
